fix(wasm): clamp DPR to stay within WebGL max texture size (4096)

Android device ALN-AL80 has 4160x2340 physical pixels, exceeding the
WebGL2 max texture size of 4096. This caused a fatal wgpu validation
error on Surface::configure.

- start_app() now accepts max_dpr parameter (f32)
- JS calculates safe DPR: min(devicePixelRatio, 4096 / max_css_dim)
- Bevy Window uses scale_factor_override to enforce the limit

Multi-stage audit findings addressed:

Architecture (MAJOR):

  (eliminates 20+ scattered .join('.claude') chains)

  in CWD deletion/rename protection — prevents bypass via command
  substitution

Parity (CRITICAL):
- Power steering: implement Python bridge delegation instead of stub
- Session start version check: delegate to Python bridge instead of
  returning None
- Session start hook migration: actually remove global hooks via
  Python bridge instead of just detecting them
- Preference parsing: support both table format and ### header format
  for Python parity
- Lock counter file extension: .json → .txt for cross-version compat

Quality (MINOR):
- Add re-exports to amplihack-hooks lib.rs
- Document all intentional error swallowing with comments
- Remove unsafe env::set_var from tests (use pre-seeded counters
  and default-based tests instead)
- Remove unused imports (Arc, Barrier, Path)

All 91 tests pass. Clippy clean. Fmt clean.

* feat: Phase 2 — CLI + launcher crate with clap, ManagedChild, signals, nesting detection

Phase 2 implementation:

2a — CLI command parsing with clap derive:
- Full CLI struct with all subcommands (Install, Uninstall, Launch,
  Claude, Copilot, Codex, Amplifier, Plugin, Memory, Recipe, Mode, Version)
- Sub-enums for Plugin (install/uninstall/link/verify),
  Memory (tree/export/import/clean), Recipe (run/list/validate/show),
  Mode (detect/to-plugin/to-local)
- Python delegation via subprocess for memory, recipe, plugin, mode commands
- Command dispatch module routing parsed args to handlers

2b — Launcher + process management:
- ManagedChild with bounded Drop (SIGTERM → 3s wait → SIGKILL)
- Signal handling via signal-hook (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP → AtomicBool)
- Child processes in own process group via setpgid (prevents double-SIGINT)
- Binary finder with PATH search, env override, version detection
- Type-safe EnvBuilder with set-based PATH deduplication
- Settings manager using AtomicJsonFile for atomic settings.json CRUD

2c — Nesting detection:
- Primary: AMPLIHACK_SESSION_ID env var check
- Secondary: Session file (PID + timestamp) in ~/.claude/runtime/
- Stale detection: dead PID + >1h old = stale (ignored)
- Session claim/release for lifecycle management

Also updates bins/amplihack to use the new CLI crate and adds
workspace deps (clap, signal-hook, libc).

35 new tests across all modules.

commit.message:
Frontend-driven dimensions: resize command, derived picker row count

The frontend measures its character grid size (cols × rows) using a probe
element and sends it to core. Core uses these dimensions for everything:
viewport, picker result count, pane rects.

Rust backend:
- initial_render(cols, rows): accepts dimensions from frontend on load
- resize(cols, rows): handles window resize, stores dimensions, re-renders
- ViewportSize stored alongside EditorState, used by emit_render
- No more hardcoded 120×40

Frontend:
- measureCharSize(): creates a hidden probe span, measures char width and
  line height from the actual font
- computeGridSize(): derives cols/rows from window.innerWidth/Height
- Window resize debounced at 50ms, sends resize command to Rust
- Initial render sends measured dimensions

Core (render.rs):
- Picker result count derived from render height:
  picker_height = 70% of screen, max_results = picker_height - 4
  (header, input, status, border). Adapts to any window size.
- Replaces hardcoded take(50)

commit.message:
Fix Windows atomic ops to return pre-operation value (match Linux/C11 semantics) (#812)

* Initial plan

* fix: use InterlockedExchangeAdd for Windows atomic ops to return old value

Replace InterlockedAdd/InterlockedIncrement/InterlockedDecrement with
InterlockedExchangeAdd which returns the value before the operation,
matching the behavior of __sync_fetch_and_add (GCC) and
atomic_fetch_add (C11).

Also fix ATOMIC_SUB macro parenthesization: -(n) instead of -n.

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prefer GCC builtins over Interlocked for MinGW compatibility

Reorder #ifdef checks so GCC-based compilers (including MinGW) use
__sync_fetch_and_add builtins via a direct __GNUC__ version check,
instead of relying on __GNUC_PREREQ which is broken on MinGW.

Only pure MSVC now uses InterlockedExchangeAdd for atomic operations.
This completely avoids any MinGW compatibility concerns with Windows
Interlocked functions.

commit.message:
Android: fix treble clef missing on staff

Android system fonts ship NotoSansSymbols-Regular-Subsetted.ttf which
has the Musical Symbols Unicode block (U+1D11E) stripped out. Neither
Compose TextMeasurer nor native Canvas drawText can render the clef.

Fix: render the clef once on the dev machine (Segoe UI Symbol via
sharp/SVG) and bundle the result as a drawable-nodpi PNG asset.
MusicStaff.kt loads it with BitmapFactory and scales it dynamically
to match the current lineSpacing (clefH = 7 × lineSpacing, aspect-
ratio preserved), so it renders correctly at all screen densities.

Also fix leftMargin: was hardcoded 60px (only ~20dp at xxhdpi);
now lineSpacing × 4.2 so the clef area scales with density.

commit.message:
Fix Directory_Delete_MountVolume test: guard Unmount calls with Directory.Exists check (#125348)

`finally` blocks in Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, and 3.2 unconditionally called
`MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName)`. When `Directory.Delete` removes
the mount point directory as part of the delete operation, the
subsequent `Unmount` call throws (Win32 error 4390/3), gets caught by
the scenario's `catch`, and sets `s_pass = false`.

## Changes

- **`Delete_MountVolume.cs` — Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, 3.2 `finally`
blocks**: Wrap `Unmount`/`DeleteDir` calls with
`Directory.Exists(mountedDirName)`, matching the pattern already used in
Scenarios 3.3–3.5.

```csharp
// Before
finally
{
    MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
    DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
}

// After
finally
{
    if (Directory.Exists(mountedDirName))
    {
        MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
        DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
    }
}
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problem

The `Directory_Delete_MountVolume.RunTest()` test in
`src/libraries/System.Runtime/tests/System.IO.FileSystem.Tests/Directory/Delete_MountVolume.cs`
fails because the `finally` blocks in Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, and 3.2
unconditionally call `MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName)`, but
`Directory.Delete` may have already removed the mount point directory.
When `Unmount` tries to call `DeleteVolumeMountPoint` on a path that no
longer exists, it throws (Win32 error 4390 or 3), which gets caught by
the scenario's `catch` block and sets `s_pass = false`, failing the
test.

Scenarios 3.3, 3.4, and 3.5 already correctly guard their cleanup with
`if (Directory.Exists(mountedDirName))` before attempting to unmount.
The fix is to apply the same pattern to the 4 `finally` blocks that are
missing it.

## Fix

Change the `finally` blocks in Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, and 3.2 to guard the
`Unmount` and `DeleteDir` calls with `Directory.Exists(mountedDirName)`,
matching the existing pattern used in Scenarios 3.3–3.5.

There are exactly 4 `finally` blocks to change. Each one currently looks
like:

```csharp
finally
{
    MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
    DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
}
```

Each should be changed to:

```csharp
finally
{
    if (Directory.Exists(mountedDirName))
    {
        MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
        DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
    }
}
```

The 4 locations in the file
`src/libraries/System.Runtime/tests/System.IO.FileSystem.Tests/Directory/Delete_MountVolume.cs`
are:

1. **Scenario 1 finally** (around lines 80–84)
2. **Scenario 2 finally** (around lines 125–129)
3. **Scenario 3.1 finally** (around lines 167–171)
4. **Scenario 3.2 finally** (around lines 209–213)

**Do not change anything else.** No changes to `s_pass`, `Eval`, method
signatures, test structure, or any other lines. Only add the
`Directory.Exists` guard to these 4 `finally` blocks.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat
exploration (may be truncated):

User: explain this test to me and what s_pass asserts

Assistant: ## Explanation of `Delete_MountVolume.RunTest()`

This is a Windows-specific test that verifies `Directory.Delete` works
correctly when **NTFS volume mount points** are involved. A volume mount
point is a directory that acts as an entry point to another volume
(similar to a drive letter, but mapped to a folder).

### What It Tests

The test runs **7 scenarios**, all variations of the same theme —
deleting directories that are accessed through or contain mounted
volumes:

| Scenario | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **1** | A *different* drive is mounted onto the current drive. Delete
a directory on the other drive *via* the mount point. |
| **2** | The *current* drive is mounted onto a different drive. Delete
a directory on the current drive *via* the mount point on the other
drive. |
| **3.1** | The current drive is mounted onto *itself* (at the root).
Delete a directory via that self-referencing mount. |
| **3.2** | The current drive is mounted onto the *current working
directory*. Delete via that mount. |
| **3.3** | ⚠️ Delete the **mounted directory itself** (the mount
point). Verifies the mount point is removed without nuking the
underlying volume. |
| **3.4** | ⚠️ Delete the **parent directory** of a mount point (parent
also has other files/dirs). Verifies recursive delete handles mount
points inside the tree. |
| **3.5** | Same as 3.4 but targets a *different* subdirectory as the
mount point within the tree. |

### The Pattern in Each Scenario

1. **Create** a directory to serve as a mount point.
2. **Mount** a volume onto that directory using `MountHelper.Mount`.
3. **Create** a directory tree (files and subdirectories) on the source
volume.
4. **Delete** the tree — either via the mount path or by deleting a
parent that contains the mount.
5. **Verify** the directory no longer exists.
6. **Clean up** — unmount and delete the mount directory in a `finally`
block.

### What `s_pass` Asserts

`s_pass` is a **boolean accumulator** for the overall test result. It
starts as `true` and is set to `false` in two situations:

1. **Any `Eval(...)` call returns `false`** — `Eval` checks a condition
(e.g., "directory was deleted") and sets `s_pass = false` if the
condition fails, logging the error.
2. **Any scenario throws an unhandled exception** — each `catch` block
sets `s_pass = false` and logs the error.

At the very end, in the outermost `finally`:

```csharp
Assert.True(s_pass);
```

This is a **single assertion that summarizes all scenarios**. If *any*
scenario failed a condition or threw an exception, `s_pass` will be
`false` and the test fails. This pattern was common in older test code —
it lets the test run all scenarios even if one fails (rather than
stopping at the first `Assert` failure), collecting all errors before
reporting.

### Why It's Disabled

The `[ActiveIssue("https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/14378")]`
attribute means this...

commit.message:
Cap picker results at 50 — eliminates serialization bottleneck

The picker was sending all 10K+ page results in the RenderFrame JSON.
On a large vault, serializing and transmitting thousands of PickerRow
objects via IPC caused a visible delay on picker open.

Only ~10 rows are visible in the picker. Capped at 50 (enough for
keyboard scrolling) with .take(50). The TUI's ratatui renderer already
clips to the visible area, so this was only hurting the GUI's JSON
serialization path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(telegram/bindings): polyglot TelegramContext for Go, Rust, Kotlin, Java, D, V

Implement Message snapshot + Context handler argument + Client wrapper
for all 6 non-Python language bindings, all binding directly to libtg
(the TDLib C wrapper) via each language's native FFI mechanism.

Changes:
- go/telegram.go: full CGO bindings — Message, Client, Context, filter
  constants, on_message/on_edited registration via C bridge, goroutine-
  safe handler dispatch
- rust/src/lib.rs: rewritten with tg_bindings (from tg.h); Message,
  MediaType, Client, Context<'a>, static handler registry, extern C
  message_bridge; removed old zsys_core wrappers
- rust/build.rs + Cargo.toml: updated to bind tg.h / link libtg
- kotlin/Telegram.kt: JNA bindings — TgMessage data class,
  TelegramContext, TelegramClient with onMessage/onEdited, bridge
  callback via ConcurrentHashMap<Long, handler>
- java/Telegram.java: JNA bindings — TgMessage, TelegramContext,
  TelegramClient (AutoCloseable), Java-style Consumer<TelegramContext>
  handlers
- d/telegram.d: extern(C) declarations + D wrapper Message struct,
  Context struct with reply/edit/delete methods, Client struct with
  RAII destructor and on_message/on_edited, extern(C) bridge callback
- v/telegram.v: V module with C declarations, Message struct, Context
  struct with ! error-return methods, Client struct, __global handler
  registry and bridge_callback

All languages mirror the Python TelegramContext API:
  ctx.chat_id / ctx.reply_to_id / ctx.is_reply / ctx.arg / ctx.has_args
  ctx.has_media / ctx.is_photo / ctx.is_video / ... / ctx.is_dice
  ctx.reply(text) / ctx.edit(text) / ctx.delete() / ctx.react(emoji)

fix: エスケープシーケンス問題の根本的解決（precmd/PROMPT_COMMAND ドレインへ移行） (#102)

* fix: エスケープシーケンス問題の根本的解決（precmd/PROMPT_COMMAND ドレインへ移行）

- dot_zshrc: p10k instant_prompt 初期化を .zshrc 先頭に追加
  - キャッシュが存在する場合のみ有効化（初回は graceful skip）
  - p10k が OSC 10/11 クエリを適切に処理するため漏れを根本的に防止
- dot_zshrc: 起動時ドレインを precmd 関数（_drain_esc_responses）に置き換え
  - 以前の read -t 0.1 は .zshrc 先頭で実行されていたが、p10k は
    .zshrc.d/ の末尾で読み込まれるためドレイン後に OSC 11 クエリが
    発生しており効果がなかった（根本原因）
  - precmd に移行することで全初期化完了後かつプロンプト表示前に毎回
    実行され、あらゆるタイミングの応答を確実に捕捉できる
  - -t 0（非ブロッキング）使用によりプロンプト表示への遅延なし
- dot_bashrc: 起動時ドレインを PROMPT_COMMAND ドレインに置き換え
  - zsh と同様の理由で precmd 相当の PROMPT_COMMAND に移行
  - 既存の PROMPT_COMMAND がある場合も安全に連結
- dot_vimrc: set background=dark を追加
  - vim/neovim の OSC 11 クエリ（背景色問い合わせ）の発生を抑制
  - rgb:cccc/cccc/cccc 応答の発生源を根本から排除

Close #101

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: OSC 11 クエリを環境変数で事前に抑止する

COLORFGBG と POWERLEVEL9K_TERM_BACKGROUND を設定することで、
p10k・vim・neovim 等のツールが OSC 11 クエリを送信する前に
背景色情報を提供し、クエリ自体を防止する。

【変更内容】
- dot_zshrc: COLORFGBG と POWERLEVEL9K_TERM_BACKGROUND を PATH 設定直後に追加
  - COLORFGBG=15;0（白前景/黒背景=ダーク）: vim 等の OSC 11 クエリ抑止
  - POWERLEVEL9K_TERM_BACKGROUND=0: p10k 専用の背景色明示指定
  - export により非インタラクティブな子プロセス（自動化スクリプト）にも継承
- dot_bashrc: COLORFGBG を interactive チェック直後に追加
  - bash セッション起動時から vim 等への OSC 11 抑止が有効になる
  - 自動化スクリプトに export で継承されるため自動化処理への混入も防止
- dot_tmux.conf: update-environment に COLORFGBG を追加
  - クライアント再接続時も COLORFGBG が tmux セッションに伝播する

【根拠】
既存のドレイン処理（precmd/_drain_esc_responses）は「応答が来た後の処理」であり、
非インタラクティブな自動化スクリプトでは機能しない。
OSC 11 クエリ自体を送らせないことが唯一の根本解決策。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Incremental DOM renderer — persistent elements, diff-based patching

Replace full-rebuild rendering with an incremental approach:

Pane lines:
- Line divs are created once and reused across frames.
- ensureLineEls adds/removes at the end when line count changes.
- On each frame, each line is diffed against the previous frame:
  - Text identical + same source: only toggle cursor-line class (2 DOM ops)
  - Text changed: update one line's innerHTML + gutter text
- Status bar only updated when cursor/mode/title/dirty changed.

Picker overlay:
- Picker container created on open, removed on close (not rebuilt per frame).
- Picker rows are persistent divs, patched incrementally:
  - Same label/middle/right: only toggle selected class + marker text
  - Content changed: rebuild that row's innerHTML
- Header only updated when query/title changes.
- Preview/status only updated when text content differs.

Performance improvement:
- j/k cursor move: ~0.1ms (2 classList.toggle) vs 76ms (full rebuild)
- Insert char: ~1ms (1 line innerHTML) vs 76ms
- Picker navigate: ~0.1ms (2 row class toggles) vs 76ms
- Scroll: ~5ms (all lines update) — same as before but no DOM creation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 76ms DOM render: single innerHTML write instead of 100+ createElement calls

The old approach created DOM nodes one by one (createElement + appendChild
for every line, span, gutter). For 20 lines with styled spans, that was
100+ individual DOM mutations — 76ms per frame.

New approach: build the entire frame as an HTML string, then set
editorEl.innerHTML once. One DOM mutation, one reflow. For 20 lines,
this should be < 2ms.

Also: reuse a single div element for escapeHtml instead of creating
a new one per call.

commit.message:
Fix connect timeout not accounting for DNS resolution time (#810)

* Initial plan

* Fix connect timeout not accounting for DNS resolution time

The ConnectTimeout function previously only applied the timeout to the
TCP connect phase (poll/select), but DNS resolution via getaddrinfo()
had no timeout applied. On platforms like Android with no network,
getaddrinfo() can block for 30-40 seconds, making user-set
connect_timeout and timeout values ineffective.

Changes:
- ConnectTimeout now tracks elapsed time for DNS resolution and
  subtracts it from the TCP connect timeout. If DNS takes longer
  than the timeout, it returns ETIMEDOUT immediately.
- http_client_connect adjusts SSL handshake timeout by subtracting
  time already spent on DNS+TCP connect.
- http_client_exec checks overall request timeout after connect,
  handling cases where connect took longer than expected.
- Updated build files (CMakeLists.txt, Makefile) to link htime.c
  with all targets that use hsocket.c.

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: use consistent error handling and fail immediately on SSL timeout

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use CHECK_TIMEOUT after connect and add hsocket.c+htime.c to nslookup target

- Replace inline timeout check with CHECK_TIMEOUT macro by moving
  variable declarations before the connect: label to avoid crossing
  initializations with goto.
- Add hsocket.c and htime.c to nslookup CMake target for Windows
  link compatibility (dns.c calls WSAInit from hsocket.c).

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add explicit closesocket(connfd) in ConnectTimeout before returning -ETIMEDOUT

When DNS resolution exhausts the timeout budget, explicitly close the
socket with closesocket() and return -ETIMEDOUT directly, instead of
relying on socket_errno_negative() which reads errno indirectly and
could return the wrong error code on Windows.

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
